title: cerbera floribunda
intro: Cerbera floribunda, also known as the sea mango or fish poison tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of the Indo-Pacific and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The extracts from the plant have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, alleviating fever, and protecting against certain diseases. Cerbera floribunda is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
